etymology.description: semantic 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") + phonetic 奇(奇) (qí) -> to ride horseback
etymology.interpretation: The character 騎(騎) (qí, "to ride") combines the semantic component 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") to indicate the concept of riding, specifically horseback riding, with the phonetic component 奇(奇) (qí) providing the pronunciation.
etymology.simplification: 騎(騎) was simplified to 骑(騎) by replacing 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") with a simpler form 马(馬).

examples.chinese: 我每天骑自行车上学(我每天騎自行車上學)。
examples.english: I ride my bicycle to school every day.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢骑马(他喜歡騎馬)，感受自然的美丽(感受自然的美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuan qí mǎ, gǎnshòu zìrán de měilì.
examples.english: He likes to ride horses and enjoy the beauty of nature.

examples.chinese: 在周末，我们经常去乡下骑车(在週末，我們經常去鄉下騎車)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhōumò, wǒmen jīngcháng qù xiāngxià qí chē.
examples.english: On weekends, we often go to the countryside to ride bikes.
